claim.statement: On client-side route changes in single-page apps, programmatically move focus to the new view (typically its first unique heading or main landmark) so keyboard and screen-reader users are not stranded on stale or detached elements, preserving a sensible focus order per WCAG 2.4.3.

limitations: Only move focus in response to a user-initiated navigation to avoid surprising focus jumps (SC 3.2)

do: Set focus to the new view's unique heading or main region after navigation
Make the focus target programmatically focusable (tabindex=-1 if non-interactive)
Scroll the focused element into view and keep it clear of sticky overlays
avoid: Leaving focus on the now-removed trigger or at the top of a detached DOM
Moving focus on events the user did not initiate
